task_completion.description: Log in to Splunk using the provided credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk web interface visible in a browser, indicating a successful login (e.g., Splunk home page or search app visible).
Dashboard creation dialog or the dashboard view itself displaying the title 'Targeted_User_Investigation'.

task_completion.description: Create a panel displaying top targeted users based on failed logins in 'index=security_logs'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dashboard panel or search interface showing a query with 'index=security_logs' and an aggregation by user (e.g., 'top user' or 'stats count by user'), displaying user data.

task_completion.description: Configure the first panel to set a token named 'clicked_user' upon selection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Drilldown configuration menu for the first panel showing a 'Set Token' action with the token name 'clicked_user', or the SimpleXML source showing <set token="clicked_user">.

task_completion.description: Create a second panel displaying attacking IPs filtered by the '$clicked_user$' token.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second dashboard panel or its search configuration showing a query that includes the token '$clicked_user$' and displays IP addresses (e.g., timeline or statistics of IPs).

integrity.description: The agent used the Splunk Web GUI to create the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Splunk web interface in a browser, rather than using terminal commands to generate the dashboard.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Splunk application on localhost:8000.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar shows localhost:8000 and the standard Splunk Enterprise interface is visible.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply write the dashboard XML in an external text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Interactions occur within the browser (either the Splunk UI dashboard builder or the Splunk SimpleXML source editor), not in external editors like nano or gedit.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: Dashboard name must be 'Targeted_User_Investigation'. Token name must be 'clicked_user' (referenced as '$clicked_user$' in the second panel). The index used in the first panel must be 'security_logs'. Splunk username is 'admin' and password is 'SplunkAdmin1!'.
